_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **ACTION REQUIRED — Confirm 2026 annual LL97 compliance report filing:** The workspace must verify whether any NYC facility it occupies or manages (or on whose behalf it files) exceeds 25,000 gross square feet and falls on the DOB Sustainability Laws Covered Buildings List (CBL, published March 2026). The standard annual reporting deadline is **May 1** each year (reflecting prior-year energy data), with a 60-day grace period to **June 30**. For 2026 filings, an extension to **August 29, 2026** is available via the BEAM portal by June 30, 2026, for a **$60 fee**. Failure to file triggers a late penalty of **$0.50 per square foot per month** until submission — $50,000/month for a 100,000 sq ft facility. *Source: NYC Accelerator LL97 Page, NYC Accelerator / NYC DOB, 2026. https://accelerator.nyc/ll97; NYC DOB LL97 Page, NYC DOB, March 2026. https://www.nyc.gov/site/buildings/codes/ll97-greenhouse-gas-emissions-reductions.page*

- **ACTION REQUIRED — Mandatory ESPM property-type reporting as of 2026:** Beginning with calendar year 2026 reporting, all covered building owners **must** classify and report emissions using Energy Star Portfolio Manager (ESPM) property types — the option to use original NYC Building Code occupancy groups expired after 2025. The workspace must confirm that any BEAM portal submissions for covered NYC premises use ESPM-aligned property types and that DOB NOW, ESPM, and BEAM accounts are linked under the same credentials. *Source: NYC DOB LL97 Buildings Emissions Limits Page, NYC DOB. https://www.nyc.gov/site/buildings/codes/ll97-buildings-emissions-limits.page*

- **WINDOW CLOSING — Verify Good Faith Effort (GFE) and decarbonization plan status for any non-compliant covered premises:** For buildings that filed GFE decarbonization plans by May 1, 2025, required capital improvements must have been completed by **May 1, 2026** to retain penalty mitigation. Buildings that missed this deadline face **50% higher penalty rates ($404/tonne vs. $268/tonne)** starting in 2027. If the workspace leases space in a covered building, it should review lease LL97 cost-allocation clauses immediately, as landlords are increasingly inserting clauses passing excess emissions charges to tenants. *Source: Insparisk LL97 Compliance Guide, Insparisk, February 2026. https://insparisk.com/nyc/ll97-compliance-guide; Carter Ledyard & Milburn LL97 Advisory, Carter Ledyard & Milburn LLP, March 2024. https://www.clm.com/local-law-97-what-nyc-building-owners-and-operators-need-to-know-now-that-the-laws-first-compliance-period-2024-2029-has-begun/*

- **COST ALERT — 2030 compliance period will impose ~74% stricter limits on warehouse/storage facilities:** Workspaces operating or leasing warehouse/distribution/cargo storage space in NYC classified as Storage Group S face a drop in the emissions intensity cap from **0.00426 tCO2e per sq ft (2024–2029)** to approximately **0.00111 tCO2e per sq ft (2030–2034)** — a ~74% tightening. A 150,000 sq ft distribution centre's annual allowance falls from 639 tCO2e to ~167 tCO2e; penalty exposure at $268/tonne could exceed $250,000/year if no retrofits are made. Begin multi-year capital planning now: construction timelines, permitting, and equipment lead times can easily consume 3–4 years. *Source: Envigilance LL97 Warehouse Guide, Envigilance, January 2026. https://envigilance.com/energy-monitoring/nyc-building-energy-laws/local-law-97/ll97-warehouse/*

- **MONITORING — Track 2030 compliance gap for all NYC covered premises:** Approximately 57% of covered buildings are currently projected to exceed their 2030–2034 caps based on 2024 benchmarking data. The workspace should commission an energy audit and LL97 gap analysis for every NYC building in its operational footprint, benchmarked against the 2030 limits, and model retrofit scenarios. *Source: NYC Accelerator LL97 Page, NYC Accelerator / NYC DOB, 2026. https://accelerator.nyc/ll97*
